heading: Credit for production of clean hydrogen
label: 45V Credit for production of clean hydrogen

children.text: the kilograms of qualified clean hydrogen produced by the taxpayer during such taxable year at a qualified clean hydrogen production facility during the 10-year period beginning on the date such facility was originally placed in service, multiplied by
the applicable amount (as determined under subsection (b)) with respect to such hydrogen.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a)(2), the applicable amount shall be an amount equal to the applicable percentage of $0.60. If any amount as determined under the preceding sentence is not a multiple of 0.1 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.1 cent.

children.label: i
children.text: not greater than 4 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 2.5 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen,

children.label: i
children.text: less than 2.5 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 1.5 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen,

children.label: i
children.text: less than 1.5 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen, and

children.label: ii
children.text: not less than 0.45 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen,

children.label: D
children.text: In the case of any qualified clean hydrogen which is produced through a process that results in a lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions rate of less than 0.45 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen, the applicable percentage shall be 100 percent.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: 3 Inflation adjustment
children.text: The $0.60 amount in paragraph (1) shall be adjusted by multiplying such amount by the inflation adjustment factor (as determined under section 45(e)(2), determined by substituting “2022” for “1992” in subparagraph (B) thereof) for the calendar year in which the qualified clean hydrogen is produced. If any amount as increased under the preceding sentence is not a multiple of 0.1 cent, such amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of 0.1 cent.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the term “lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions” has the same meaning given such term under subparagraph (H) of section 211()(1) of the Clean Air Act (()(1)), as in effect on the date of enactment of this section.oo42 U.S.C. 7545

children.heading: GREET model
children.label: B GREET model
children.text: The term “lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions” shall only include emissions through the point of production (well-to-gate), as determined under the most recent Greenhouse gases, Regulated Emissions, and Energy use in Transportation model (commonly referred to as the “GREET model”) developed by Argonne National Laboratory, or a successor model (as determined by the Secretary).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified clean hydrogen” means hydrogen which is produced through a process that results in a lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions rate of not greater than 4 kilograms of CO2e per kilogram of hydrogen.

children.label: I
children.text: in the United States (as defined in section 638(1)) or a possession of the United States (as defined in section 638(2)),

children.label: II
children.text: in the ordinary course of a trade or business of the taxpayer, and

children.label: III
children.text: for sale or use, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the production and sale or use of such hydrogen is verified by an unrelated party.

children.heading: Provisional emissions rate
children.label: C Provisional emissions rate
children.text: In the case of any hydrogen for which a lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions rate has not been determined for purposes of this section, a taxpayer producing such hydrogen may file a petition with the Secretary for determination of the lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions rate with respect to such hydrogen.

children.label: A
children.text: owned by the taxpayer,

children.label: B
children.text: which produces qualified clean hydrogen, and

children.label: C
children.text: the construction of which begins before .January 1, 2028

children.heading: Treatment of facilities owned by more than 1 taxpayer
children.label: 1 Treatment of facilities owned by more than 1 taxpayer
children.text: Rules similar to the rules section 45(e)(3) shall apply for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Coordination with credit for carbon oxide sequestration
children.label: 2 Coordination with credit for carbon oxide sequestration
children.text: No credit shall be allowed under this section with respect to any qualified clean hydrogen produced at a facility which includes carbon capture equipment for which a credit is allowed to any taxpayer under section 45Q for the taxable year or any prior taxable year.

children.heading: Credit reduced for tax-exempt bonds
children.label: 3 Credit reduced for tax-exempt bonds
children.text: Rules similar to the rule under section 45(b)(3) shall apply for purposes of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: was originally placed in service before , and, prior to the modification described in subparagraph (B), did not produce qualified clean hydrogen, andJanuary 1, 2023

children.label: i
children.text: is modified to produce qualified clean hydrogen, and

children.label: ii
children.text: amounts paid or incurred with respect to such modification are properly chargeable to capital account of the taxpayer,

children.heading: Increased credit amount for qualified clean hydrogen production facilities
children.label: e Increased credit amount for qualified clean hydrogen production facilities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any qualified clean hydrogen production facility which satisfies the requirements of paragraph (2), the amount of the credit determined under subsection (a) with respect to qualified clean hydrogen described in subsection (b)(2) shall be equal to such amount (determined without regard to this sentence) multiplied by 5.

children.label: i
children.text: the construction of which begins prior to the date that is 60 days after the Secretary publishes guidance with respect to the requirements of paragraphs (3)(A) and (4), and

children.label: ii
children.text: which meets the requirements of paragraph (3)(A) with respect to alteration or repair of such facility which occurs after such date.

children.label: B
children.text: A facility which satisfies the requirements of paragraphs (3)(A) and (4).

children.label: i
children.text: the construction of such facility, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to any taxable year, for any portion of such taxable year which is within the period described in subsection (a)(2), the alteration or repair of such facility,

children.heading: Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.label: B Correction and penalty related to failure to satisfy wage requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(7)(B) shall apply.

children.heading: Apprenticeship requirements
children.label: 4 Apprenticeship requirements
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 45(b)(8) shall apply.

children.heading: Regulations and guidance
children.label: 5 Regulations and guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall issue such regulations or other guidance as the Secretary determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations or other guidance which provides for requirements for recordkeeping or information reporting for purposes of administering the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: f Regulations
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of enactment of this section, the Secretary shall issue regulations or other guidance to carry out the purposes of this section, including regulations or other guidance for determining lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions.
